purpose: This repository contains Infrastructure as Code (IaC) configurations for Cross River Bank's Cos Lending Selling platform. It automates the provisioning and management of AWS cloud resources including Aurora databases, Airflow workflows, API services, and storage solutions across multiple environments.

apis.purpose: Web application interface for lending services
Administrative interface for lending system management

dataEntities.name: DatabaseCredentials
dataEntities.description: Secure access credentials for database connections

dataEntities.name: ApplicationUser
dataEntities.description: User identity and permissions for application access

messagingPatterns.name: SQS Queues
messagingPatterns.description: Message queuing for selling-x service integration

messagingPatterns.name: CloudWatch Events
messagingPatterns.description: System monitoring and alerts for platform operations

findings.title: Hardcoded SQL permissions in rotation scripts
findings.description: The database credential rotation scripts (ai-api and db-external-access modules) contain hardcoded SQL permissions that are granted to users. These should be parameterized based on environment to prevent potential privilege escalation.

findings.title: State bucket name generation creates potential collision risk
findings.description: The terraform state bucket name is generated based on branch names, which could lead to collisions or unauthorized access. Consider using a more robust naming strategy with additional entropy and access controls.
